prompt: I have a simulator for an AR(2) time series process: y_t = phi1 * y_{t-1} + phi2 * y_{t-2} + noise(sigma). I want to use BayesFlow to do amortized inference on the three parameters (phi1, phi2, sigma) from observed sequences of length 100. The ordering of observations matters here — this is temporal, not exchangeable. Set up the workflow with the right architecture.
assertions: Defines a simulator that generates AR(2) time series of length 100
Routes the time series through summary_variables (not inference_conditions)
Does NOT use SetTransformer for the time series data
Uses TimeSeriesTransformer, TimeSeriesNetwork, or FusionTransformer as the summary network
Calls .constrain('sigma', lower=0) for the noise standard deviation
Does NOT flatten the time series into a single vector for inference_conditions
Starts with the Small model configuration from references/model-sizes.md
Passes validation_data= to the fit_* call
Saves and inspects training history (JSON + inspect_training or equivalent)
Runs in-silico diagnostics and checks against house thresholds
Accesses posterior samples by original names (phi1, phi2, sigma), NOT 'inference_variables'
